name: Groove HQ Helpdesk API
what_it_does: Shared inbox and helpdesk platform designed for small businesses — with REST API for managing conversations, customers, and agents, providing email-first customer support without the complexity of enterprise helpdesk systems.
use_cases: Creating support conversations from external events or monitoring systems via API
Querying customer conversation history for CRM enrichment agents
Assigning and routing tickets based on external business logic
Retrieving support metrics and conversation data for reporting workflows
Syncing customer data between Groove and other business tools
not_for: Large support teams needing enterprise SLA management and complex workflows
Multi-channel support with WhatsApp and social media (Groove is email-focused)
ITSM and IT service desk use cases
best_when: A small business team needs simple shared email inbox with API access for basic integration needs without the cost or complexity of Zendesk.
avoid_when: You need enterprise features, complex workflow automation, or multi-channel support beyond email and live chat.

auth.notes: Access token from Groove OAuth2 flow or personal access token from account settings. Bearer token authentication. Account-scoped access. OAuth2 for third-party integrations.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starter from $12/agent/month; Pro from $22; Company from $34
pricing.notes: Affordable per-agent pricing targeting small businesses. Simple, clean interface. Bootstrapped company — focused on sustainable product development. Good alternative to expensive enterprise tools.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply; documented in API reference

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covers conversations, customers, agents, and mailboxes. Webhook support for conversation events. Simpler API surface than enterprise tools. No official SDK — standard HTTP calls.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Conversation creation creates new items. Update operations are idempotent. Read operations always idempotent. No explicit idempotency key support.
known_agent_gotchas: Smaller API surface area than enterprise tools — some operations UI-only
Rate limits not clearly documented — implement conservative retry logic
OAuth2 requires full flow for third-party apps; personal tokens simpler for agents
Webhook payloads may have breaking changes between API versions

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. OAuth2 and token auth. GDPR compliant. US data residency. Small team product — security practices not as formally certified as enterprise tools.
